textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value:
Take any course under this prof, his slides are very well written, course content is just right, easy to study, lectures are easy to understand and intuitive. Prof is literally a "chill guy". Easy quizzes and exams, straight from the course content (well explained in slides). Microeconomics as a course is very simple, involves basic math, and interesting concepts. MUST DO.
Tip: He'll most likely take the Game Theory HUL in the coming even (confirm this) sem.
Cheers!

textBlocks.label: Content sharing
textBlocks.value: Exam relevant content is not shared, but general content is shared

textBlocks.label: Grading
textBlocks.value: Easy if you attend classes, difficult otherwise

textBlocks.label: Overall
textBlocks.value:
The course has no enforced attendance at all, but most of the course content is not available from any other sources than the lectures. Lecture slides with minimal content(not enough to do well in exams) are shared, and a lot of the questions that come in the exam are from topics strictly mentioned in class and not present in the lecture slides. Attending classes and minimal preparation is all you need for an easy 9/10, but it is hard to get more than a 7/8 without attending classes religiously (Don't pick this course if you don't plan to attend classes, irrespective of the attendance policy)
Grading for the course is moderate to good grading, and the course content involves minimal mathematics, is easy to grasp, and interesting.

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value: Nobody got grade below B (except for 2 students afaik). No quizzes or surprise quiz. Only minor, major and attendance marks. Only drawback is morning classes. No attendance in tut(movies are shown in tut btw). Prof is one of the most chill profs. Only one question was asked in minor and major. Prof asked us to write only 3- 4 lines for the question in 20 minutes. He even told us which question will be asked in the exam. Best HUL course.

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value:
Relatively more course components: surprise quiz, class participation has high weightage, act presentation, major minor
Taking notes of all discussions in class is important, reading the text and having opinions and inferences after thinking about it is imp, no hw as such other than reading material, if paid attention in class no extra worload / effort required, attendance criteria scrictly followed, teaches well but is strict, good experience

textBlocks.label: Evaluations used

textBlocks.label: Content sharing
textBlocks.value: Exam relevant content is not shared, but general content is shared

textBlocks.label: Overall
textBlocks.value: Attendance is mandatory, and important, since a lot of the exam questions come from topics discussed at length in the lectures, and you're expected to present the same reasoning and ideas discussed in class, which are subjective and impossible to arrive at if you haven't attended class. The course readings are interesting, and the course will be very easy to manage if you are an avid reader. If you have a good grasp of English and have no issues writing essay-length answers for hours, the exams will be easy too.

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value: 20 marks were about drawing a poster and that was very uncertain for me. Not strict attendance but 5 marks for class participation, well which requires to be present in class

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value: Teaching and content were okayish but Grading was bad - only 2-3 people got A out of 600 people. moreover in minor and major exams, if you write the exact words in prof's slides, then only he would give you marks else even if your answer is correct, he would dismiss it saying "very Generic answer"

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value:
The course policy was too vague for non minor and major portion, it was a significant part which was VERY unplanned. They had research activities which were only needed for their own study purposes which were graded for us, and we got a very short prior notice to attend these activities in the tutorial timings, very unplanned, very unprofessional, especially the TAs
The prof on contrary is an absolute pookie and teaches really very well. The exams were so no difficult, easy 9 if gone through the slides just once acche se
If taking this course, please make sure TAs are not from the 2024-2025 Sem 2 batch

Content (rating): 3.0/5 Workload: 2.0/5 Grading: 4.0/5 Attendance leniency: 1.0/5 Overall: 3.0/5 Attendance in first 5 min, regular surprise quizzes, and grading - absolute. Rest content was moderate, some reading based essay type question in exam.

textBlocks.label: Comments
textBlocks.value: Grading was absolute (A : 80, A- : 72, B : 64, and so on). Attendance 75%+ mandatory to sit for major exams. But there were 3 bonus marks for attendance as well (lec + tut). Class is interactive, major minor mcq based in moodle, subjective surprise quizzes (best 2 out of 5). 25% of course load was a group ppt that took a lot of effort and time. 40% major, 25% minor, 10% surpise quiz. Lots of pdf to read.
